designPreferences.desiredFeel:
Established, blue-collar-professional. Texas without kitsch. Should 
  feel like a thirty-year local business, not a tech startup or a 
  national franchise.
- Color: deep navy + warm white + one accent (Texas-flag red or a warm 
  orange) reserved exclusively for primary CTAs.
- Typography: a strong humanist sans (Inter or Plus Jakarta) for body; 
  a sturdy display face for headlines. No script or decorative fonts.
- Photography: real crew, real trucks, real Huntsville. Until supplied, 
  use neutral high-quality placeholders labeled "REPLACE WITH REAL 
  PHOTO." No stock images of generic movers in white shirts.
- No carousels, no parallax, no auto-playing video, no chat widget, no 
  newsletter signup, no popups, no social icons in the header.
Build a phone-first, mobile-first website for Huntsville Moving Inc., a 
family-owned Texas moving company in business since 1992, owned and 
operated by James and Yvonne Yaggi, based in Huntsville, TX. The single 
primary conversion is a phone call to (936) 291-7230, which is answered 
by the owner. Every layout, CTA, and trust element should serve that 
call. A short estimate form exists only as a secondary path for 
after-hours leads.

PAGES TO BUILD:
Home · Local Movers (Huntsville, TX) · Long-Distance Moving Across 
Texas · Home & Apartment Moves · Office & Commercial Moves · Packing & 
Unpacking · Piano, Antique & Specialty Item Moving · Labor-Only Loading 
& Unloading · Areas We Serve (hub) · One city page each for Huntsville, 
College Station, Bryan, Madisonville, Trinity, Conroe, The Woodlands, 
Willis, Livingston, Navasota · About · Reviews · FAQs · Free Estimate · 
Contact · Blog.

SERVICE LABELS (use exactly; do not preserve the old labels):
- Local Movers (replaces "Local Moving Services")
- Long-Distance Moving Across Texas (replaces "Long Distance Moving")
- Home & Apartment Moves (replaces "Residential Moving")
- Office & Commercial Moves (replaces "Commercial & Office Moving")
- Packing & Unpacking (absorbs the old "Moving Boxes & Supplies" page)
- Piano, Antique & Specialty Item Moving (replaces "Specialty Item 
  Moving" and the orphaned "Furniture Moving" page)
- Labor-Only Loading & Unloading (new — currently only buried in FAQ)
designPreferences.desiredFeatures:
CONVERSION RULES (apply site-wide):
- The phone number (936) 291-7230 is the largest, most repeated element 
  on every page. Treat it as a brand mark.
- Two CTA verbs only, site-wide: "Call" (primary) and "Request a Free 
  Estimate" (secondary). Wording can vary; destinations cannot.
- Mobile sticky bottom bar on every page: full-width "Call 
  (936) 291-7230" button as the dominant action, with a smaller "Text 
  Us" or "Request Estimate" secondary link beside it.
- Desktop sticky header: phone number top-right at the largest size 
  type allows, with microcopy beneath: "Call — answered by James, owner 
  since 2006."
- No quote form in the homepage hero. The estimate form lives on 
  /free-estimate and is linked, not embedded, from the homepage hero.
- Every phone number on the site is a tel: link. Every "Text Us" link 
  is an sms: link to the same number.

GLOBAL ELEMENTS:
- Header: logo left, primary nav center, oversized tap-to-call phone 
  right. No social icons in the header.
- Trust strip directly under every page hero: Google star rating + 
  review count linked to GMB · Licensed · Insured · Bonded · TxDMV # · 
  USDOT # · Family-Owned Since 1992.
- Footer: complete NAP with full street address, phone, hours 
  (Mon–Sat 8a–6p, Sunday closed), TxDMV and USDOT numbers, full 
  service list, full city list, social links, current year, privacy and 
  terms.
- Breadcrumbs on every interior page.
- Schema where relevant: LocalBusiness/MovingCompany, BreadcrumbList, 
  Service, FAQPage, Review/AggregateRating.

HOMEPAGE SECTION ORDER:
1) Hero: real photo of the actual crew with a Huntsville Moving truck 
   (placeholder until supplied). One-line headline: "Family-Owned 
   Huntsville Movers, Trusted Since 1992." Subhead naming the footprint: 
   "Serving Huntsville, Walker County, and the Sam Houston / 
   Bryan-College Station corridor." Two CTAs — "Call (936) 291-7230" 
   (primary) and "Request a Free Estimate" (secondary). Star rating + 
   review count above the fold.
2) Trust strip (license numbers, insured/bonded, since 1992).
3) "Talk to James, owner since 2006" band: small portrait of James, 
   one short owner-voiced paragraph, repeated phone CTA. This replaces 
   the homepage form.
4) Services grid — six cards: Local Movers, Long-Distance Moving Across 
   Texas, Home & Apartment Moves, Office & Commercial Moves, Packing & 
   Unpacking, Piano/Antique & Specialty. Each card has a relevant icon 
   (no generic placeholders), a one-line scope statement, and a "Call 
   about [service]" link.
5) "How a move with us works" — four numbered steps: (1) Call for a 
   free estimate, (2) Walk-through and plan, (3) Move day, (4) Settled 
   in. Each step is one short paragraph in owner voice.
6) Reviews block: embedded star rating, three to five verbatim Google 
   reviews with real names, "Read all reviews on Google" link.
7) Service area block: Texas inset map highlighting Walker, Montgomery, 
   Madison, Trinity, and Brazos counties; clickable list of every city 
   page; one-line fallback: "Don't see your city? Call us — we move 
   across Texas."
8) FAQ accordion, six questions with FAQPage schema: cost factors, 
   lead time, insurance coverage, deposit policy, last-minute 
   availability, labor-only loading.
9) Final CTA band: oversized phone number, "Open Mon–Sat 8a–6p," and a 
   secondary "Request a Free Estimate" link.
